_specialist_shape_comment: When ticket_type is `specialist` or `prerequisite`, set `specialist` to an object: { type: 'ui'|'a11y'|'data'|'perf'|'sec'|'infra'|'test'|'obs'|'prereq', agent: 'claude'|'codex'|'any', reason: 'short why' }. Leave as null for primary tickets.
title: Replace with ticket title
summary: Replace with the smallest reviewable slice for this ticket.

validation._evidence_shape_comment: Optional/additive. Older tickets may omit evidence. Record only smallest sufficient validation evidence selected by the risk matrix; examples: command transcripts, adapter intents, artifact paths, bounded log excerpts, screenshots/browser journey refs, metric/trace summaries, selected taste-invariant evidence, and skipped evidence with reason/residual risk. Avoid secrets, production data, unbounded logs, and unnecessary retention.

prompt_handoff.notes: Each role should read the upstream `## Next Agent Prompt (...)` section, validate the required fields, and log gaps if incomplete. Prompt Packet field names match `state.json` field names (e.g. `stage`, not `current_stage`). If the human types only a minimal continuation prompt such as `Proceed`, `Continue`, `Progress`, `Next action`, or `Go ahead`, follow workflow-reference.md Universal "Proceed" routing: resolve the active ticket, read next_actor and the latest relevant Prompt Packet, then load the corresponding skill or role. Prerequisite sub-tickets and specialist sub-tickets may add `ticket_type`, `execution_mode`, `waiting_on`, `completion_rule`, `true_blocker_definition`, `first_questions`, `next_skill`, `specialist.type`, `specialist.skill`, `specialist.agent`, and `specialist.reason` when useful. Reviewer handoffs should also include `review.current_pass`, pass status, current PR head SHA when known, and `next_skill` when changes are requested.

entries.notes: example Codex fallback captured usage via scripts/codex_session_tokens.py local JSONL parsing; use codex resume --all only as a human/manual fallback when JSONL parsing is unavailable
